extraction.one_line_summary: BODNAR Maciej won the Stage 20 time trial by 1 second over KWIATKOWSKI Michał, while FROOME Chris extended his GC lead from 23 to 54 seconds to virtually clinch the Tour.
tactical_patterns: BARDET Romain took excessive cornering risks and overcooked the flat sections before the climb, suffering badly uphill when it mattered.
FROOME Chris rode tactically conservative on the climb after dominating early splits, preserving energy rather than maximizing time gains once advantage was clear.
LANDA Mikel finished 15th in TT despite GC support role, positioning just 1 second behind BARDET for podium without team clearance.
MATTHEWS Michael and team SUNWEB targeted polka dot sprint points in mountain stages to weaken DE GENDT Thomas's jersey defense.

rider_signals.BODNAR Maciej: positive: First Tour stage win at 32 suggests late-career TT specialist can peak for single-day targets
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Extended 23-second lead to 54 seconds in Stage 20 TT, demonstrating decisive time trial superiority when marginal GC defense required
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: Took dangerous corners and overextended on flats before climb, visibly suffering uphill and watching podium spot evaporate to LANDA
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: 15th place TT finish while in GC support role reveals untapped podium capacity, now 1 second from BARDET's third place
rider_signals.URÁN Rigoberto: neutral: Knew time trial limitations before stage but lacked sufficient GC buffer, finishing 2:20 behind FROOME overall
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Overcame 100-point deficit week prior to secure green jersey through SUNWEB's coordinated mountain sprint attacks
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: negative: Won five stages and led green jersey but lost 100-point advantage as MATTHEWS targeted intermediate sprints in mountains
